response:
In C# it is possible to measure the time it takes for a UDP packet to reach a server  
but it is not straightforward  
UDP does not guarantee delivery or timing information  
To measure the round trip time you would need to send a packet and wait for a response  
But if you only want to measure the one way time you would need to use a different approach  
One way to do this is to use the System.Net.Sockets.UdpClient class  
You can send a packet and then use a Stopwatch to measure the time taken  
However you cannot know how long it took for the packet to reach the server  
Because there is no confirmation of receipt  
You can only know the time it took to send the packet  
And the time it took to receive a response if one is sent back  
So in summary measuring one way UDP packet time is not possible  
But you can measure the time it took to send the packet and wait for a response  
If you need to measure the time it took for the packet to reach the server  
You would need to use a different protocol such as TCP  
Or implement a custom solution where the server sends a response  
This would allow you to measure the round trip time  
But not the one way time
